The adversary candidate fixes a real gap (conditional_sources schema) but introduces a regression (implicit stopping rule). The exploit is not strong enough to rank above the student candidate overall: the student scores higher on approach-step precision, MCP fallback completeness, and the original 6 engineer-prompt risks. The adversary wins only on output-format rows, which are a minority of the dataset. The plausible exploit space for this candidate is largely exhausted: no stronger single exploit was found that would fool the judge across the majority of training and validation rows. The student candidate is the stronger choice.
